manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: waitress, manger, wildlife, bra, liter, ravine, exam, deficit, mumps.
Your opponent's team words are: impostor, groundhog, ignorance, info, hypocrite, graveyard, psychopath, income.
Distractor words are: broker, raincoat, allergy, tact, armpit, nighttime, vinegar.
The kill words are: peninsula.

CLUE: interview
TARGETS: waitress, manager, exam

We need to produce a single-word clue for our team (the user presumably is the "spymaster" for their team). They need to give clue and list of target words from our team's list (the words we want partner to guess). The clue must be related to some subset of our team's words. The format: "CLUE: <WORD>\nTARGETS: <TARGETS>"

We have to obey that the clue word cannot be one of the words in any lists (including opponent or distractor or kill words), also cannot contain parts of them. The clue must be semantically related.

Thus we need to think about possible associations: waitress, manager, wildlife, bra, liter, ravine, exam, deficit, mumps.

We need to find a common concept that can group several of these. We want as many as possible, but also it's not too obscure. Could be something like "job"? That would relate to waitress and manager. Could also be "exam" (test), "deficit" is financial. "wildlife" is animals. "bra" is clothing. "liter" is measurement. "ravine" is geography. "mumps" is disease.

"public"? hmm.

We could also try to choose two or three words only to make it easier. But we may want more words to speed up. However if we include more, it's riskier for partner to guess correctly.

But note: If partner guesses incorrectly an opponent's word or distractor, their turn ends and the other team gets a turn. But they also might guess our target words correctly. We want them to guess all our words eventually; they can guess as many as they can in one turn. But there's no limit on number of guesses per turn. So we can give clue that covers multiple target words, they can try to guess each one until they get one wrong? Actually standard Codenames rules: The spymaster gives a clue and a number (like "animal 3"). The team guesses up to that number + 1 (or unlimited?). In this variant described: "If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately." So they can keep guessing until they either guess a wrong word or decide to stop?
